headline: Retrieval primitives broke out across the quarter as new model releases and agent platforms pushed code generation and evaluation toward production.
body: Vector search, HNSW, and semantic chunking moved into breakout territory together, signaling rotation toward retrieval infrastructure, while spec-driven development, debugging, deterministic behavior, event logging, and game loop entered as new breakout techniques. On the model side, Anthropic shipped Claude Sonnet 5, which closes the benchmark gap with Opus 4.8, and released Claude Science in beta with reproducible workflows and code generation for scientific computing. Agent tooling advanced through Google Cloud's continuous evaluation pipelines for multi-agent systems on Gemini and tutorials on building agents with Claude Code. AI-assisted development, code generation, and MLLM-as-judge held as leading lagging techniques, with code generation showing the strongest ratio at 78.

cited_items.title: Sonnet 5 is LIVE And It Competes With Opus
How to build a continuous evaluation pipeline for multi-agent systems with Gemini
How to Build an AI Agent with Claude Code (Claude AI Agent Tutorial)
